title: पार्वती मङ्गल
devanagariText:
॥ श्री पार्वती मङ्गल ॥

॥ गोस्वामी तुलसीदासकृत ॥

॥ मङ्गलाचरण ॥
श्रीगणेशाय नमः। श्रीजानकीवल्लभो विजयते। श्रीरामाय नमः॥

जय गणपति सदगुण सदन कविवर बदन कृपाल।
बिघ्न हरण मङ्गल करण जय जय गिरिजालाल॥ १॥

जय जय जय गिरिराजकिशोरी। जय महेश मुख चन्द्र चकोरी॥
जय गजबदन षडानन माता। जगत जननि दामिनि दुति गाता॥ २॥

॥ शिवपार्वती विवाह वर्णन ॥
हिमगिरि गृह प्रगटीं भवानी। अतुलित रूप तेज बल बानी॥
तन पर भाँति भूषण बनाये। मनहुँ सकल सोभा लहि आये॥ ३॥

शिव विवाह हित करहिं उपाई। मैना हिमगिरि बड़ सुख पाई॥
बनी बरात चली अति भारी। देखत पंथ पुरीजन नारी॥ ४॥

सजे सिंगार शंभु तहँ आये। देवन्ह जय जय जय सुख गाये॥
बाजहिं बाजन बिबिध प्रकारा। हरषहिं सुर मुनि सकल संसारा॥ ५॥

सखीं गौरि मङ्गल गावहीं। सुन्दर रचि बनाय लावहीं॥
पानिगहन भा तब सम्मत सकल। होइ सुनी देखी पारबती अमल॥ ६॥

बेद मन्त्र मुनिवर उचरहीं। शिव गौरी हरषित मन करहीं॥
भवानी शंकर भये बिवाही। अति सुख मंगल गान बजाही॥ ७॥

सुर मुनि सकल करहिं जय जय कारा। कुम्भज रिषि करि विधि विस्तारा॥
लावा होम करहिं बिधि नाना। वेद मन्त्र ध्वनि कुम्भज ग्याना॥ ८॥

॥ फेरे ॥
सात फेर लागे सुहावन। शिव गौरी मन अति सुख पावन॥
बेद रीति सबु कीन्ह बिबाहू। शिव पारबती परम सनेहू॥ ९॥

॥ आशीर्वाद ॥
सब विधि सकल मंगल सोई। जगत पिता जगत माता दोई॥
शिव पारबती चरित सुहावा। सुनत सकल मंगल सुख पावा॥ १०॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
पारबती मंगल जो गावै। सो सदा सकल सुख पावै॥
बिवाह मंगल अति सुखदाई। तुलसीदास हरषित मन गाई॥

सुनत पढ़त गुनत सुभ नीके। शिव-पारबती करहिं सदा ठीके॥
विधि वन्दना विवाह जो गावै। मनबाञ्छित वर नारि सो पावै॥

॥ इति श्री गोस्वामी तुलसीदासकृत पार्वती मङ्गल सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Parvati Mangal ||

|| Goswami Tulsidasakrit ||

|| Mangalacharan ||
Shri Ganeshaya Namah. Shri Janaki-vallabho Vijayate. Shri Ramaya Namah.

Jaya Ganapati Sadguna Sadana Kavivara Badana Kripala.
Bighna Harana Mangala Karana Jaya Jaya Girijaalala. || 1 ||

Jaya Jaya Jaya Giriraja-kishori. Jaya Mahesha Mukha Chandra Chakori.
Jaya Gajabadana Shadanana Mata. Jagata Janani Damini Duti Gata. || 2 ||

|| Shiva-Parvati Vivaha Varnan ||
Himagiri Griha Pragatin Bhavani. Atulita Rupa Teja Bala Bani.
Tana Para Bhanti Bhushana Banaye. Manahun Sakala Sobha Lahi Aaye. || 3 ||

Shiva Vivaha Hita Karahin Upai. Maina Himagiri Bada Sukha Pai.
Bani Barata Chali Ati Bhari. Dekhata Pantha Purijana Nari. || 4 ||

Saje Singara Shambhu Tahan Aaye. Devanah Jaya Jaya Jaya Sukha Gaye.
Bajahin Bajana Bividha Prakara. Harashahin Sura Muni Sakala Sansara. || 5 ||

Sakheen Gauri Mangala Gavahin. Sundara Rachi Banaya Lavahin.
Panigrahana Bha Taba Sammata Sakala. Hoi Suni Dekhi Parbati Amala. || 6 ||

Veda Mantra Munivara Ucharahin. Shiva Gauri Harashita Mana Karahin.
Bhavani Shankara Bhaye Bivahi. Ati Sukha Mangala Gana Bajahi. || 7 ||

Sura Muni Sakala Karahin Jaya Jaya Kara. Kumbhaja Rishi Kari Bidhi Vistara.
Lava Homa Karahin Bidhi Nana. Veda Mantra Dhvani Kumbhaja Gyana. || 8 ||

|| Phere ||
Sata Phera Lage Suhavana. Shiva Gauri Mana Ati Sukha Pavana.
Veda Riti Sabu Kinha Bivahu. Shiva Parbati Parama Snehu. || 9 ||

|| Ashirvad ||
Saba Vidhi Sakala Mangala Soi. Jagata Pita Jagata Mata Doi.
Shiva Parbati Charita Suhava. Sunata Sakala Mangala Sukha Pava. || 10 ||

|| Phalashruti ||
Parbati Mangala Jo Gavai. So Sada Sakala Sukha Pavai.
Bivaha Mangala Ati Sukhadai. Tulasidasa Harashita Mana Gai.

Sunata Padhata Gunata Subha Nike. Shiva-Parbati Karahin Sada Thike.
Vidhi Vandana Vivaha Jo Gavai. Manabanchhita Vara Nari So Pavai.

|| Iti Shri Goswami Tulsidasakrit Parvati Mangal Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
पार्वती मङ्गल गोस्वामी तुलसीदास की रचना है जिसमें भगवान शिव और माता पार्वती के विवाह का सुन्दर वर्णन है।

मङ्गलाचरण में गणेश और गिरिजा (पार्वती) की वन्दना है। पार्वती को गिरिराजकिशोरी, महेश-मुखचन्द्र-चकोरी और जगत-जननी कहा गया है।

विवाह वर्णन में हिमालय के गृह में भवानी के प्रकट होने, उनके अतुलित रूप-तेज, शिव की बारात, सिंगार, पाणिग्रहण, वेदमन्त्रों के उच्चारण, सात फेरों और विवाह-सम्पन्न होने का अत्यन्त सुन्दर चित्रण है।

फलश्रुति में कहा गया है कि जो पार्वती मङ्गल गाता है, वह सदा सकल सुख पाता है। विवाह के अवसर पर इसका पाठ और गायन करने से मनवाञ्छित वर या वधू प्राप्त होती है। शिव-पार्वती सदा प्रसन्न होकर कृपा करते हैं।
benefits: Tulsidas's beautiful composition describing Lord Shiva and Parvati's wedding
Recitation brings marital harmony and bliss in married life
Listening or reading helps unmarried find ideal life partners
Sung during Hindu weddings for auspiciousness and divine blessings
Invokes the grace of the cosmic parents - Shiva and Parvati
Traditionally associated with all-round happiness and removes obstacles in marriage
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Parvati, Parvati Mangal (पार्वती मङ्गल) stands as a sacred prayer. Drawn from Parvati Mangal - Goswami Tulsidas, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to bring all-round happiness and remove obstacles in marriage and recitation brings marital harmony and bliss in married life. It is also said to sung during Hindu weddings for auspiciousness and divine blessings. For best results, devotees chant this on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri. The text contains 10 verses and takes around 15 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Parvati Mangal in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is Parvati Mangal by Goswami Tulsidas?
seoFaqs.answer: Parvati Mangal (पार्वती मङ्गल) is a celebrated devotional composition by Goswami Tulsidas (1532-1623 CE) — the great Awadhi poet-saint who composed the Ramcharitmanas. Written in beautiful Awadhi-Hindi with mangal-geet (auspicious-song) metres, the work poetically narrates the divine wedding of Lord Shiva (Mahadev/Shankar) and Mata Parvati (Girija/Bhavani — daughter of Himalaya). The composition begins with mangalacharan invocations to Ganesha and Parvati (as Girijaalala), describes Parvati's birth in the Himalaya household, the proposal and wedding-procession (barat) led by Shiva from Kailash, the saptapadi (seven-vows around fire), and concludes with phala-shruti promising those who recite Parvati Mangal at weddings or seeking marriage receive shubh-vivah blessings. The text is preserved in classical Tulsidas tradition. (Source: Parvati Mangal — Goswami Tulsidas.)

seoFaqs.question: Who was Goswami Tulsidas and what are his major works?
seoFaqs.answer: Goswami Tulsidas (1532-1623 CE) was the greatest Hindi-Awadhi devotional poet of medieval India — born at Rajapur (or Soron) on the banks of Yamuna, abandoned at birth and raised by Sage Narharidas. He is regarded by Vaishnavas as an avatara of the Sanskrit poet Valmiki (composer of the original Sanskrit Ramayana) sent to compose the Rama-charita in vernacular Awadhi for common people. His major works include: (1) Ramcharitmanas — the most-recited Hindu epic in North India (Awadhi version of Valmiki Ramayana); (2) Vinaya Patrika — devotional petition-songs to Rama; (3) Hanuman Chalisa — the 40-verse Hanuman hymn recited daily by 100+ million Hindus; (4) Hanuman Bahuk; (5) Dohavali; (6) Kavitavali; (7) Geetavali; (8) Krishnavali; (9) Janaki Mangal — wedding of Sita-Rama; (10) Parvati Mangal — wedding of Shiva-Parvati; (11) Vairagya Sandipani; (12) Barvai Ramayana. His Ramcharitmanas single-handedly shaped North Indian devotional culture and Hindi literature. (Sources: Tulsidas hagiography; Bhaktamala by Nabhadas.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the story of Shiva-Parvati's wedding narrated in Parvati Mangal?
seoFaqs.answer: Parvati Mangal poetically condenses the elaborate Shiva-Parvati wedding story from the Shiva Purana, Skanda Purana, and Padma Purana: (1) Birth: Goddess Sati, after self-immolating at Daksha's yajna, was reborn as Parvati to Himavan (king of Himalayas) and Mena/Maina; (2) Penance: Parvati performed extreme tapas to win Shiva — eating only leaves (Aparna), then nothing, standing in cold water, surrounded by five fires; (3) Test by Shiva: Shiva visited her in disguise as a Brahmin to test her resolve — she passed; (4) Proposal: Saptarishis (seven sages) approached Himavan as Shiva's emissaries to formally propose marriage; (5) Wedding-Procession: Shiva's barat from Kailash was extraordinary — Brahma as priest, Vishnu as elder, ganas-bhutas-pishachas as wedding-party, Nandi as vahana, Mena initially terrified by Shiva's terrible-form, then pacified when He showed His handsome bridegroom-form; (6) Saptapadi: Seven steps around the sacred fire while Sage Agastya (Kumbhaja-rishi) chanted Veda-mantras; (7) Aashirvad: All devas-rishis blessed the cosmic-couple as Jagat-Pita-Mata (universe's father-mother). (Sources: Shiva Purana Rudra Samhita; Parvati Mangal text; Skanda Purana Kashi Khanda.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Parvati Mangal recited at Hindu weddings?
seoFaqs.answer: Parvati Mangal is one of the most-recited mangal-geet at Hindu weddings (especially in North India, Awadh region, Bihar, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh) for several reasons: (1) Cosmic Model: Shiva-Parvati's wedding is the prototype of all Hindu weddings — every bride is invoked as Parvati and every groom as Shiva during the saptapadi; (2) Auspiciousness: The very name mangal means auspicious — recitation invokes wedding-day shubha-shakti; (3) Tulsidas's composition is in Awadhi (the popular language) making it accessible to all village-women who sing it during pre-wedding ceremonies, mehendi, sangeet, vidaai, and griha-pravesh; (4) Phala-shruti promises that unmarried young women who hear/recite Parvati Mangal receive their desired husband (man-banchhita-vara), unmarried young men receive their ideal wife (man-banchhita-nari), and married couples receive marital-harmony, mutual-love, and longevity; (5) The composition models ideal husband-wife relationship — Shiva the renunciate yet devoted to Parvati, Parvati the supreme goddess yet humble before Shiva. (Sources: phala-shruti verses; North Indian wedding traditions.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should Parvati Mangal be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Parvati Mangal is most powerfully recited on Monday (Somvar — Shiva's day), every Pradosh-vrat (13th tithi sunset), Maha Shivaratri (Phalguna Krishna Chaturdashi — the night of Shiva-Parvati's wedding), Hartalika Teej (Bhadrapada Shukla Tritiya — when Parvati performed tapas for Shiva), the entire Shravan month (sacred to Shiva), every Monday of Shravan (Shravan Somvar), Karwa Chauth and Vat Savitri (women's vrats for husband's longevity), Gauri Tritiya, before any Hindu wedding (during the entire week of marriage rituals), at the wedding-mandap during pheras, by unmarried girls seeking ideal husbands, by women whose marriages face challenges, and at Annapurna or Parvati temple visits. The proper procedure: bathe, wear yellow or red clothes, light a deepa before Shiva-Parvati image, offer bel-patra, white flowers, dhoop, and recite the entire Parvati Mangal aloud or in sangeet/group-singing format. (Sources: Tulsidas tradition; North Indian wedding paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does Parvati Mangal recitation bestow?
seoFaqs.answer: The phala-shruti of Parvati Mangal — Sunata padhata gunata subha nike, Shiva-Parbati karahin sada thike, Vidhi vandana vivaha jo gavai, Manabanchhita vara nari so pavai — promises remarkable benefits: (1) Shiva-Parvati's grace constantly upon the household (sada thike); (2) Manvanchhita-vara — desired husband for unmarried girls; (3) Manvanchhita-nari — desired wife for unmarried men; (4) Marital-harmony and bliss in married life; (5) Removal of obstacles in marriage (vivah-baadha) — particularly powerful for those facing late-marriage, broken-engagements, family-opposition, or financial-obstacles to wedding; (6) Children and family-prosperity; (7) Removal of Manglik dosha (Mars-affliction) and other Kuja-Shukra astrological obstacles to marriage; (8) Husband-longevity for married women (similar to Karwa Chauth, Vat Savitri); (9) For widows/divorcees — peace and remarriage opportunities; (10) For those involved in wedding-businesses (caterers, decorators, priests, mehendi artists) — success in profession; (11) Ultimately spiritual-marriage of jivatma with Paramatma (Shiva-Shakti union as moksha). (Sources: Parvati Mangal phala-shruti; Tulsidas tradition.)
